claims.summary: cautilus version --verbose is the quickest binary-only summary of what Cautilus currently considers its product surface (who it is for, which normalization family or eval surface to start from, and which report fields matter when a run rejects).
claims.verbatim: `cautilus version --verbose` is also the quickest binary-only summary of what
claims.edgeRationale: version provenance is part of readiness reporting (S2) but also reports the installed product surface that S1 establishes.

claims.summary: Legacy Homebrew installs are not a supported update channel anymore; they must be removed and reinstalled through install.sh instead of mixing channels.
claims.verbatim: Legacy Homebrew installs are not a supported update channel anymore; remove them and reinstall through `install.sh` instead of mixing channels.

claims.summary: cautilus init adapter --repo-root . generates a minimal repo-local adapter scaffold.
claims.verbatim: # generate a minimal repo-local adapter scaffold

claims.summary: cautilus doctor adapter --repo-root . resolves and validates the adapter.
claims.verbatim: # resolve and validate the adapter

claims.summary: Each shipped starter under examples/starters/ ships a pre-filled cautilus-adapter.yaml, a canonical input fixture, and a README that explains what to replace next.
claims.verbatim: Each starter ships a pre-filled `cautilus-adapter.yaml`, a canonical input fixture, and a README that explains what to replace next.

claims.summary: Starter placeholders are bootstrap help, not proof that a real consumer workflow is already wired.
claims.verbatim: Treat starter placeholders as bootstrap help, not as proof that a real consumer workflow is already wired.

claims.summary: A minimal adapter must wire at least one real runnable path to reach doctor ready.
claims.verbatim: A minimal adapter must wire at least one real runnable path to reach `doctor ready`:
claims.edgeRationale: The runnable-path requirement gates readiness (S2) and constrains the adapter bootstrap surface that S1 installs.

claims.summary: For codex_exec, --codex-home-mode isolated keeps user config and session state out of the eval while --codex-auth-mode inherit copies only Codex auth into the isolated home.
claims.verbatim: For `codex_exec`, `--codex-home-mode isolated` keeps user config and session state out of the eval while `--codex-auth-mode inherit` copies only Codex auth into the isolated home.

claims.summary: Use --codex-auth-mode env when the wrapper intentionally relies on OPENAI_API_KEY.
claims.verbatim: Use `--codex-auth-mode env` when the wrapper intentionally relies on `OPENAI_API_KEY`.

claims.summary: When neither auth source is available, the runner reports runner_auth_missing before spending the whole live run on cases that cannot authenticate.
claims.verbatim: When neither source is available, the runner reports `runner_auth_missing` before spending the whole live run on cases that cannot authenticate.

claims.summary: When typed runners are present, evaluate fixture chooses the runner whose surfaces contains the fixture surface key.
claims.verbatim: When typed runners are present, `evaluate fixture` chooses the runner whose `surfaces` contains the fixture surface key.

claims.summary: The {runner_id} placeholder is available to command templates.
claims.verbatim: The `{runner_id}` placeholder is available to command templates.

claims.summary: If the selected runtime is fixture, the observed proof class is fixture-smoke even when the adapter declares a stronger possible runner class.
claims.verbatim: If the selected runtime is `fixture`, the observed proof class is `fixture-smoke` even when the adapter declares a stronger possible runner class.

claims.summary: For dev/repo and dev/skill, a non-fixture coding-agent runtime may report the adapter-declared coding-agent-messaging class while preserving the smoke assessment as assessment metadata, but only when the observed packet reports the matching runtime telemetry.
claims.verbatim: For `dev/repo` and `dev/skill`, a non-fixture coding-agent runtime may report the adapter-declared `coding-agent-messaging` class while preserving the smoke assessment as assessment metadata, but only when the observed packet reports the matching runtime telemetry.

claims.summary: cautilus doctor binary --json reports binary health with no repo required.
claims.verbatim: # binary health, no repo required

claims.summary: cautilus doctor commands --json performs command discovery that is safe for wrapper tooling and agent runtimes.
claims.verbatim: # command discovery, safe for wrapper tooling and agent runtimes
claims.edgeRationale: Command discovery is a readiness probe (S2) and the binary surface agent runtimes rely on for orchestration (A1).

claims.summary: cautilus doctor status provides no-input agent orientation over binary, adapter, agent-surface, and claim state.
claims.verbatim: # no-input agent orientation over binary, adapter, agent-surface, and claim state
claims.edgeRationale: doctor status is a readiness orientation probe (S2) and the agent's no-task orientation entry point (A1).

claims.summary: cautilus doctor --repo-root reports repo readiness for evaluation.
claims.verbatim: # repo readiness for evaluation

claims.summary: cautilus doctor --next-action returns one current onboarding step plus the exact continuation loop.
claims.verbatim: # one current onboarding step plus the exact continuation loop

claims.summary: cautilus doctor --scope agent-surface checks local agent-skill discoverability in a consumer repo.
claims.verbatim: # local agent-skill discoverability in a consumer repo
claims.edgeRationale: Agent-surface scope is a readiness probe (S2) over the repo-local agent surface that S1 installs.

claims.summary: cautilus discover claims performs declared-claim discovery and proof planning.
claims.verbatim: # declared-claim discovery and proof planning

claims.summary: cautilus discover scenarios --json provides a scenario-normalization catalog for agents that need proposal-input examples.
claims.verbatim: # scenario-normalization catalog, for agents that need proposal-input examples

claims.summary: cautilus <subcommand> --help exits 0 for the registered native command surface, including grouped topics such as cautilus improve search --help.
claims.verbatim: `cautilus <subcommand> --help` exits `0` for the registered native command surface, including grouped topics such as `cautilus improve search --help`.

claims.summary: Use doctor status --json when a Cautilus Agent or agent is invoked without a detailed task.
claims.verbatim: Use `doctor status --json` when a Cautilus Agent or agent is invoked without a detailed task.
claims.edgeRationale: Guidance for no-task agent invocation routes the orchestration agent (A1) to the readiness orientation probe (S2).

claims.summary: doctor status emits cautilus.agent_status.v1: a read-only orientation packet over binary health, local agent-surface readiness, adapter state, claim-state availability, scan scope, and branch choices.
claims.verbatim: It emits `cautilus.agent_status.v1`: a read-only orientation packet over binary health, local agent-surface readiness, adapter state, claim-state availability, scan scope, and branch choices.
claims.edgeRationale: The agent_status packet is a readiness orientation artifact (S2) and the branch-choice surface the orchestration agent reads (A1).

claims.summary: Use doctor --next-action when you want one current onboarding step plus the exact follow-up loop.
claims.verbatim: Use `doctor --next-action` when you want one current onboarding step plus the exact follow-up loop.

claims.summary: If repo setup is ready but runner proof is not, the doctor next action can point at runner assessment setup before the first bounded eval loop.
claims.verbatim: If repo setup is ready but runner proof is not, that next action can point at runner assessment setup before the first bounded eval loop.
claims.edgeRationale: The next-action behavior is readiness reporting (S2) that gates entry to the runner-backed evaluate loop (E1).

claims.summary: Use doctor --scope agent-surface to verify only the Cautilus Agent and local agent-surface install.
claims.verbatim: Use `doctor --scope agent-surface` to verify only the Cautilus Agent and local agent-surface install.
claims.edgeRationale: Agent-surface scope is a readiness check (S2) targeting the agent-surface install that S1 owns.

claims.summary: Use default doctor (--scope repo) to verify the repo has a real runnable evaluation path.
claims.verbatim: Use default `doctor` (`--scope repo`) to verify the repo has a real runnable evaluation path.
claims.edgeRationale: Repo-scope doctor reports readiness (S2) by verifying a runnable evaluation path the evaluate surface needs (E1).

claims.summary: Each doctor check separates stable contract from run-specific observation: id names the machine-readable readiness condition, ok reports the boolean result, meaning explains why the condition matters, and detail records the observed fact for this run.
claims.verbatim: Each `doctor` check separates stable contract from run-specific observation:

claims.summary: External executable prerequisites should put the resolved binary path in detail when present and the searched PATH in detail when missing.
claims.verbatim: External executable prerequisites should put the resolved binary path in `detail` when present and the searched `PATH` in `detail` when missing.

claims.summary: When repo-scope doctor returns ready, the JSON payload includes first_bounded_run: a starter evaluate fixture -> evaluate observation packet loop plus the scenario-normalization catalog for agents that still need proposal-input examples.
claims.verbatim: When repo-scope `doctor` returns `ready`, the JSON payload includes `first_bounded_run`: a starter `evaluate fixture -> evaluate observation` packet loop plus the scenario-normalization catalog for agents that still need proposal-input examples.
claims.edgeRationale: The ready payload is readiness output (S2) that bootstraps the first evaluate fixture/observation loop (E1).

claims.summary: When a repo intentionally keeps only named adapters under .agents/cautilus-adapters/, run cautilus doctor --repo-root --adapter-name <name> for repo-scope validation instead of expecting plain doctor to guess which named adapter you mean.
claims.verbatim: When a repo intentionally keeps only named adapters under `.agents/cautilus-adapters/`, run `cautilus doctor --repo-root /path/to/repo --adapter-name <name>` for repo-scope validation instead of expecting plain `doctor` to guess which named adapter you mean.

claims.summary: Use cautilus discover claims before writing eval fixtures when you need to inventory declared behavior claims and decide whether each belongs in human review, deterministic CI, Cautilus eval, scenario proposal work, or alignment work.
claims.verbatim: Use `cautilus discover claims` before writing eval fixtures when you need to inventory declared behavior claims and decide whether each belongs in human review, deterministic CI, Cautilus eval, scenario proposal work, or alignment work.

claims.summary: Default discovery starts from adapter-owned claim entries or README.md/AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md and follows repo-local Markdown links to depth 3.
claims.verbatim: Default discovery starts from adapter-owned claim entries or README.md/AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md and follows repo-local Markdown links to depth 3.

claims.summary: Use --previous <claims.json> --refresh-plan for deterministic refresh planning inside the same discover workflow.
claims.verbatim: Use `--previous <claims.json> --refresh-plan` for deterministic refresh planning inside the same discover workflow.

claims.summary: The refresh-plan packet includes refreshSummary, which gives agent-readable counts, changed claim source hotspots, and coordinator-facing next actions so agents do not need to infer the plan from raw packet fields.
claims.verbatim: The refresh-plan packet includes `refreshSummary`, which gives agent-readable counts, changed claim source hotspots, and coordinator-facing next actions so agents do not need to infer the plan from raw packet fields.

claims.summary: Refresh-plan output is not a saved claim map update; it must be written to a refresh-plan*.json artifact, not to the configured latest/reviewed/evidenced claim-state paths.
claims.verbatim: Refresh-plan output is not a saved claim map update; write it to a `refresh-plan*.json` artifact, not to the configured latest/reviewed/evidenced claim-state paths.

claims.summary: The CLI rejects overwriting saved claim maps with refresh-plan output because refresh-plan packets use cautilus.claim_refresh_plan.v1 while saved claim maps must remain cautilus.claim_proof_plan.v1.
claims.verbatim: The CLI rejects that overwrite because refresh-plan packets use `cautilus.claim_refresh_plan.v1`, while saved claim maps must remain `cautilus.claim_proof_plan.v1`.

claims.summary: Normal discovery output is cautilus.claim_proof_plan.v1, and it is a proof plan rather than a verdict.
claims.verbatim: Normal discovery output is `cautilus.claim_proof_plan.v1`, and it is a proof plan rather than a verdict.

claims.summary: Use cautilus discover claims status --input <claims.json> --sample-claims <n> to summarize an existing claim packet without rescanning and include bounded claim examples when an agent needs concrete candidates before choosing the next branch.
claims.verbatim: Use `cautilus discover claims status --input <claims.json> --sample-claims <n>` to summarize an existing claim packet without rescanning and include bounded claim examples when an agent needs concrete candidates before choosing the next branch.

claims.summary: The claim status summary includes gitState; when it reports isStale=true, run cautilus discover claims --previous <claims.json> --refresh-plan before review, review application, or eval planning.
claims.verbatim: The summary includes `gitState`; when it reports `isStale=true`, run `cautilus discover claims --previous <claims.json> --refresh-plan` before review, review application, or eval planning.

claims.summary: The claim status summary also includes evidenceSatisfaction so satisfied claims and their evidence refs are visible without reading the full claim packet first.
claims.verbatim: The summary also includes `evidenceSatisfaction` so satisfied claims and their evidence refs are visible without reading the full claim packet first.

claims.summary: gitState.isStale tracks recorded claim-source changes, not every HEAD change.
claims.verbatim: `gitState.isStale` tracks recorded claim-source changes, not every HEAD change.

claims.summary: If a commit only adds or updates generated claim artifacts, gitState reports headDrift=true with comparisonStatus=fresh-with-head-drift and review or eval planning may continue.
claims.verbatim: If a commit only adds or updates generated claim artifacts, `gitState` reports `headDrift=true` with `comparisonStatus=fresh-with-head-drift` and review or eval planning may continue.

claims.summary: Use cautilus discover claims extraction-input to emit the deterministic agent-extraction input packet: sources with content hashes, the embedded extraction template with version and hash, merged classification hints, and bounds.
claims.verbatim: Use `cautilus discover claims extraction-input --repo-root . --output <extraction-input.json>` to emit the deterministic agent-extraction input packet: sources with content hashes, the embedded extraction template with version and hash, merged classification hints, and bounds.
claims.edgeRationale: The extraction-input packet is a discovery surface (D1) that feeds the agent's curation/extraction pass (A2).

claims.summary: Use cautilus discover claims apply-extraction to validate an agent extraction result, anchor every verbatim excerpt, and compose the extractionMode: agent proof plan.
claims.verbatim: Use `cautilus discover claims apply-extraction --repo-root . --input <extraction-input.json> --result <extraction-result.json> --output <claims-agent.json>` to validate an agent extraction result, anchor every verbatim excerpt, and compose the `extractionMode: agent` proof plan.
claims.edgeRationale: apply-extraction is a deterministic discovery composer (D1) that validates the agent's curated extraction output (A2).

claims.summary: Neither extraction-input nor apply-extraction calls an LLM; unanchored, unbounded, or out-of-scope claims are rejected into extractionAudit.rejectedClaims instead of being silently dropped.
claims.verbatim: Neither command calls an LLM; unanchored, unbounded, or out-of-scope claims are rejected into `extractionAudit.rejectedClaims` instead of being silently dropped.

claims.summary: Use cautilus discover claims review-input --claims <claims.json> to prepare bounded deterministic review clusters for an agent or subagent.
claims.verbatim: Use `cautilus discover claims review-input --claims <claims.json>` to prepare bounded deterministic review clusters for an agent or subagent.
claims.edgeRationale: review-input is a deterministic review-prep surface (D2) that feeds the agent-driven structured review (A2).

claims.summary: discover claims review-input emits cautilus.claim_review_input.v1 and does not call an LLM or mark claims satisfied.
claims.verbatim: It emits `cautilus.claim_review_input.v1` and does not call an LLM or mark claims satisfied.

claims.summary: When adapter evidence_roots contain claim evidence bundles for known claim ids, review input may include possibleEvidenceRefs; these are reviewer hints only.
claims.verbatim: When adapter `evidence_roots` contain claim evidence bundles for known claim ids, review input may include `possibleEvidenceRefs`; these are reviewer hints only.

claims.summary: Claims already marked evidenceStatus=satisfied or already reviewed are skipped by default and recorded under skippedClaims so reviewer budget stays focused on unresolved heuristic claims.
claims.verbatim: Claims already marked `evidenceStatus=satisfied` or already reviewed are skipped by default and recorded under `skippedClaims` so reviewer budget stays focused on unresolved heuristic claims.

claims.summary: Use cautilus discover claims apply-review to merge reviewed labels and evidence refs back into a claim packet.
claims.verbatim: Use `cautilus discover claims apply-review --claims <claims.json> --review-result <review-result.json>` to merge reviewed labels and evidence refs back into a claim packet.

claims.summary: apply-review emits an updated claim packet and rejects evidenceStatus=satisfied unless a direct or verified evidence ref supports the claim.
claims.verbatim: It emits an updated claim packet and rejects `evidenceStatus=satisfied` unless a direct or verified evidence ref supports the claim.

claims.summary: Use cautilus discover claims validate to emit cautilus.claim_validation_report.v1 and fail fast on invalid packet shape or evidence refs.
claims.verbatim: Use `cautilus discover claims validate --claims <claims.json>` to emit `cautilus.claim_validation_report.v1` and fail fast on invalid packet shape or evidence refs.

claims.summary: discover claims validate does not mutate the packet or search for evidence.
claims.verbatim: It does not mutate the packet or search for evidence.

claims.summary: Use cautilus evaluate claims plan after review to turn ready cautilus-eval claims into cautilus.claim_eval_plan.v1.
claims.verbatim: Use `cautilus evaluate claims plan --claims <reviewed-claims.json>` after review to turn ready `cautilus-eval` claims into `cautilus.claim_eval_plan.v1`.
claims.edgeRationale: claims plan turns reviewed claims (D2) into an eval plan for the evaluate surface (E1).

claims.summary: Claims already marked evidenceStatus=satisfied are skipped by default by evaluate claims plan because they do not need a new eval fixture plan.
claims.verbatim: Claims already marked `evidenceStatus=satisfied` are skipped by default because they do not need a new eval fixture plan.

claims.summary: The claim eval plan packet preserves claim ids, target eval surfaces, source refs, draft intents, and unresolved questions, but it deliberately does not write host-owned fixtures or runners.
claims.verbatim: That packet preserves claim ids, target eval surfaces, source refs, draft intents, and unresolved questions, but it deliberately does not write host-owned fixtures or runners.

claims.summary: discover claims review-input, discover claims apply-review, and evaluate claims plan reject stale claim packets by default; --allow-stale-claims is an explicit operator override, not the normal agent path.
claims.verbatim: `discover claims review-input`, `discover claims apply-review`, and `evaluate claims plan` reject stale claim packets by default; `--allow-stale-claims` is an explicit operator override, not the normal agent path.
claims.edgeRationale: Stale-packet rejection gates the review/refresh commands (D2) and the eval-plan command (E1) alike.

claims.summary: cautilus discover live-targets lists live consumer targets that this host repo exposes to Cautilus.
claims.verbatim: # list live consumer targets that this host repo exposes to Cautilus

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate live runs one bounded live scenario request against one selected instance.
claims.verbatim: # run one bounded live scenario request against one selected instance

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate live prepare-request-batch turns draft scenarios into a canonical live-run request batch.
claims.verbatim: # turn draft scenarios into a canonical live-run request batch
claims.edgeRationale: prepare-request-batch normalizes draft scenarios (E2) into the canonical live-run batch the evaluate surface executes (E1).

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate live scenarios runs many explicit live scenario requests against one selected instance.
claims.verbatim: # run many explicit live scenario requests against one selected instance
claims.edgeRationale: The batch live run executes requests (E1) derived from scenario work (E2).

claims.summary: An eval live instance is one live consumer target on this host that Cautilus can select by stable id.
claims.verbatim: An eval live instance is one live consumer target on this host that `Cautilus` can select by stable id.

claims.summary: cautilus discover live-targets resolves either explicit adapter instances or a consumer-owned probe command into the same cautilus.workbench_instance_catalog.v1 packet.
claims.verbatim: `cautilus discover live-targets` resolves either explicit adapter instances or a consumer-owned probe command into the same `cautilus.workbench_instance_catalog.v1` packet.

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate live takes one selected instance id plus one request packet and returns one bounded result packet.
claims.verbatim: `cautilus evaluate live` takes one selected instance id plus one request packet and returns one bounded result packet.

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate live prepare-request-batch is the agent-facing prep surface above the live seam: it turns either cautilus.scenario.v1 draft scenarios or a normalized catalog-candidate packet into a canonical cautilus.live_run_invocation_request_batch.v1 artifact.
claims.verbatim: `cautilus evaluate live prepare-request-batch` is the agent-facing prep surface above that seam: it turns either `cautilus.scenario.v1` draft scenarios or a normalized catalog-candidate packet into a canonical `cautilus.live_run_invocation_request_batch.v1` artifact.
claims.edgeRationale: prepare-request-batch is the scenario-to-batch prep surface (E2) above the live evaluate seam (E1).

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate live scenarios is the product-owned batch primitive above the live seam: it accepts an explicit cautilus.live_run_invocation_request_batch.v1 file for one selected instance, schedules the requests in-process, retries only when the batch packet asks for it and a prior attempt returned an explicit transient class, and writes one aggregated cautilus.live_run_invocation_batch_result.v1 packet.
claims.verbatim: `cautilus evaluate live scenarios` is the product-owned batch primitive above that seam: it accepts an explicit `cautilus.live_run_invocation_request_batch.v1` file for one selected instance, schedules the requests in-process, retries only when the batch packet asks for it and a prior attempt returned an explicit transient class, and writes one aggregated `cautilus.live_run_invocation_batch_result.v1` packet.

claims.summary: The product owns the packet boundary and status semantics for live runs.
claims.verbatim: The product owns the packet boundary and status semantics.

claims.summary: The consumer still owns actual launch, auth, and runtime wiring through its adapter command.
claims.verbatim: The consumer still owns actual launch, auth, and runtime wiring through its adapter command.

claims.summary: When the adapter declares consumer_single_turn_command_template, the same command can also own a product-managed multi-turn chatbot loop above a consumer-owned single-turn seam.
claims.verbatim: When the adapter declares `consumer_single_turn_command_template`, the same command can also own a product-managed multi-turn chatbot loop above a consumer-owned single-turn seam.

claims.summary: The multi-turn loop allocates one stable per-request workspace directory at <output_file>.d/workspace/ and exposes it through the {workspace_dir} placeholder across live-run command templates.
claims.verbatim: That loop now allocates one stable per-request workspace directory at `<output_file>.d/workspace/` and exposes it through the `{workspace_dir}` placeholder across live-run command templates.

claims.summary: When the adapter also declares workspace_prepare_command_template, Cautilus runs that consumer-owned prepare command once before the first turn.
claims.verbatim: When the adapter also declares `workspace_prepare_command_template`, `Cautilus` runs that consumer-owned prepare command once before the first turn.

claims.summary: When the public scenario uses simulator.kind: persona_prompt, the adapter additionally provides simulator_persona_command_template, which normally calls cautilus evaluate live persona with repo-specific backend flags.
claims.verbatim: When the public scenario uses `simulator.kind: persona_prompt`, the adapter additionally provides `simulator_persona_command_template`, which normally calls `cautilus evaluate live persona` with repo-specific backend flags.
claims.edgeRationale: Persona-prompt simulator wiring serves the live evaluate run (E1) and the scenario simulation surface (E2).

claims.summary: Persona prompt shaping and result semantics stay product-owned while backend selection stays adapter-owned.
claims.verbatim: That keeps persona prompt shaping and result semantics product-owned while backend selection stays adapter-owned.

claims.summary: When the adapter declares consumer_evaluator_command_template, Cautilus also materializes one cautilus.live_run_evaluator_input.v1 packet and expects one cautilus.live_run_evaluator_result.v1 verdict back under scenarioResult.evaluation.
claims.verbatim: When the adapter declares `consumer_evaluator_command_template`, `Cautilus` also materializes one `cautilus.live_run_evaluator_input.v1` packet and expects one `cautilus.live_run_evaluator_result.v1` verdict back under `scenarioResult.evaluation`.

claims.summary: The batch flow keeps prep and execution separate on purpose: prep owns deterministic request synthesis plus explicit filter semantics, and run-scenarios owns concurrency, retry, transient-count aggregation, and per-attempt artifact layout for an explicit batch file.
claims.verbatim: The batch flow keeps prep and execution separate on purpose: prep owns deterministic request synthesis plus explicit filter semantics, and `run-scenarios` owns concurrency, retry, transient-count aggregation, and per-attempt artifact layout for an explicit batch file.
claims.edgeRationale: The prep/execution split governs scenario request synthesis (E2) and the live batch execution surface (E1).

claims.summary: cautilus init run (via eval) starts a fresh per-run subdirectory under one stable artifact root and pins it.
claims.verbatim: # start a fresh per-run subdirectory under one stable artifact root and pin it

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate comparison prepare prepares clean baseline and candidate git worktrees for a compare run.
claims.verbatim: # prepare clean baseline and candidate git worktrees for a compare run
claims.edgeRationale: Comparison prepare sets up baseline/candidate worktrees for skill-experiment compare (E2) feeding the evaluate run (E1).

claims.summary: cautilus doctor artifacts prune prunes older Cautilus artifact bundles.
claims.verbatim: # prune older Cautilus artifact bundles

claims.summary: workspace start defaults --root to ./.cautilus/runs/ (auto-created on first use) and prints export CAUTILUS_RUN_DIR=<absolute runDir>.
claims.verbatim: `workspace start` defaults `--root` to `./.cautilus/runs/` (auto-created on first use) and prints `export CAUTILUS_RUN_DIR=<absolute runDir>`.

claims.summary: Pass --json instead of eval when a script needs the machine-readable payload from workspace start.
claims.verbatim: Pass `--json` instead of `eval` when a script needs the machine-readable payload.

claims.summary: When CAUTILUS_RUN_DIR is already pinned, downstream commands (evaluate comparison prepare, evaluate fixture) reuse that active runDir; if nothing is pinned they auto-materialize a fresh runDir and print Active run: <path> to stderr.
claims.verbatim: When `CAUTILUS_RUN_DIR` is already pinned, downstream commands (`evaluate comparison prepare`, `evaluate fixture`) reuse that active `runDir`; if nothing is pinned they auto-materialize a fresh `runDir` and print `Active run: <path>` to stderr.

claims.summary: cautilus discover scenarios normalize chatbot normalizes chatbot-style conversation summaries.
claims.verbatim: # normalize chatbot-style conversation summaries

claims.summary: cautilus discover scenarios normalize skill normalizes skill-evaluation summaries.
claims.verbatim: # normalize skill-evaluation summaries

claims.summary: cautilus discover scenarios normalize workflow normalizes durable-workflow run summaries.
claims.verbatim: # normalize durable-workflow run summaries

claims.summary: cautilus discover scenarios prepare-input assembles a proposal-input packet from split normalized source files.
claims.verbatim: # assemble a proposal-input packet from split normalized source files

claims.summary: cautilus discover scenarios propose generates a scenario proposal packet from normalized input.
claims.verbatim: # generate a scenario proposal packet from normalized input

claims.summary: cautilus discover scenarios review-conversations builds a scenario-centric conversation review packet from normalized chatbot threads.
claims.verbatim: # build a scenario-centric conversation review packet from normalized chatbot threads
claims.edgeRationale: review-conversations builds a scenario review packet (E2) that drives structured conversation review (A2).

claims.summary: cautilus discover scenarios render-conversation-review-html renders the conversation review packet into HTML.
claims.verbatim: # render the conversation review packet into HTML

claims.summary: cautilus discover scenarios summarize-telemetry summarizes scenario-level cost, token, and duration telemetry.
claims.verbatim: # summarize scenario-level cost, token, and duration telemetry

claims.summary: Every normalize command plus cautilus evaluate observation and cautilus evaluate report build accepts --example-input: it prints a minimal valid packet to stdout that can be piped back into the same command, so operators can inspect the expected shape without clicking into a fixture on GitHub.
claims.verbatim: Every normalize command plus `cautilus evaluate observation` and `cautilus evaluate report build` accepts `--example-input`: it prints a minimal valid packet to stdout that can be piped back into the same command, so operators can inspect the expected shape without clicking into a fixture on GitHub.
claims.edgeRationale: --example-input serves normalize/scenario commands (E2) and the evaluate observation/report commands (E1).

claims.summary: cautilus discover scenarios --json now exposes those same inspect commands under normalizationFamilies[*].exampleInputCli.
claims.verbatim: `cautilus discover scenarios --json` now exposes those same inspect commands under `normalizationFamilies[*].exampleInputCli`.

claims.summary: cautilus discover scenarios propose now preserves the full ranked proposals list in the canonical JSON output.
claims.verbatim: `cautilus discover scenarios propose` now preserves the full ranked `proposals` list in the canonical JSON output.

claims.summary: The propose packet also emits an attentionView, which is a bounded human-facing shortlist derived from the full ranked set.
claims.verbatim: The same packet also emits an `attentionView`, which is a bounded human-facing shortlist derived from the full ranked set.

claims.summary: cautilus discover scenarios review-conversations stays intentionally narrower than a generic audit UI.
claims.verbatim: `cautilus discover scenarios review-conversations` stays intentionally narrower than a generic audit UI.
claims.edgeRationale: The scope-boundary on review-conversations bounds the scenario review surface (E2) and the structured-review path (A2).

claims.summary: review-conversations links normalized chatbot threads to scenario proposals and coverage hints so an operator can review behavior-eval evidence without browsing every live operator turn.
claims.verbatim: It links normalized chatbot threads to scenario proposals and coverage hints so an operator can review behavior-eval evidence without browsing every live operator turn.
claims.edgeRationale: Thread-to-proposal linking builds the scenario review packet (E2) for operator structured review (A2).

claims.summary: The shipped surface is cautilus evaluate.
claims.verbatim: The shipped surface is `cautilus evaluate` (see [docs/specs/user/evaluation.spec.md](../specs/user/evaluation.spec.md)).

claims.summary: The first preset, dev / repo, replaces the prior cautilus instruction-surface commands.
claims.verbatim: The first preset, `dev / repo`, replaces the prior `cautilus instruction-surface` commands.

claims.summary: Cautilus exposes two top-level surfaces and four presets: dev / repo for repo work contracts, dev / skill for checked-in or portable development skills, app / chat for multi-turn product conversation behavior, and app / prompt for single product input/output behavior.
claims.verbatim: Cautilus exposes two top-level surfaces and four presets:

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate fixture runs a checked-in fixture through an adapter-owned runner and then evaluates the observed packet.
claims.verbatim: `cautilus evaluate fixture` runs a checked-in fixture through an adapter-owned runner and then evaluates the observed packet.

claims.summary: evaluate fixture records a top-level proof object in eval-observed.json and eval-summary.json when runner readiness can identify the proof class or assessment state.
claims.verbatim: The command records a top-level `proof` object in `eval-observed.json` and `eval-summary.json` when runner readiness can identify the proof class or assessment state.

claims.summary: When the adapter declares runner_readiness.runners, evaluate fixture selects the runner by the fixture surface key before rendering the command template.
claims.verbatim: When the adapter declares `runner_readiness.runners`, the command selects the runner by the fixture surface key before rendering the command template.

claims.summary: Fixture-runtime runs are reported as fixture-smoke; adapter-declared proof class is preserved only as declared metadata.
claims.verbatim: Fixture-runtime runs are reported as `fixture-smoke`; adapter-declared proof class is preserved only as declared metadata.

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate observation evaluates an already-observed packet without launching the runner again.
claims.verbatim: `cautilus evaluate observation` evaluates an already-observed packet without launching the runner again.

claims.summary: npm run dogfood:self is the official on-demand self-dogfood wrapper for the repo's own AGENTS.md.
claims.verbatim: # official on-demand self-dogfood wrapper for the repo's own AGENTS.md

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate skill-experiment compares one sandboxed skill-clone experiment after host-owned execution.
claims.verbatim: # compare one sandboxed skill-clone experiment after host-owned execution

claims.summary: The npm wrapper is the canonical maintainer-facing self-dogfood path for this repo.
claims.verbatim: The npm wrapper is the canonical maintainer-facing self-dogfood path for this repo.

claims.summary: The fixture variants prove the app / chat and app / prompt fixture translation, adapter runner, and evaluator packet paths.
claims.verbatim: The fixture variants prove the `app / chat` and `app / prompt` fixture translation, adapter runner, and evaluator packet paths.
claims.edgeRationale: The fixture variants are self-dogfood proof (M1) that the evaluate fixture/runner/evaluator paths line up (E1).

claims.summary: The Codex variants run the same checked-in fixtures through the Codex backend.
claims.verbatim: The Codex variants run the same checked-in fixtures through the Codex backend.
claims.edgeRationale: The Codex variant runs checked-in fixtures through a backend (E1) as self-dogfood proof (M1).

claims.summary: The Claude variants run the same fixtures through the Claude backend, proving that the app surface runner can use more than one provider backend.
claims.verbatim: The Claude variants run the same fixtures through the Claude backend, proving that the app surface runner can use more than one provider backend.
claims.edgeRationale: Multi-backend support is an evaluate-runner capability (E1) proven through the dogfood variants (M1).

claims.summary: These backend variants are not eval live product-runner proof, and their current summaries report productProofReady=false until an app-owned runner assessment exists for the selected surface.
claims.verbatim: These backend variants are not `eval live` product-runner proof, and their current summaries report `productProofReady=false` until an app-owned runner assessment exists for the selected surface.

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate skill-experiment emits cautilus.skill_clone_experiment_report.v1 with variant_ran, baseline-versus-variant delta, rubric match, source coverage delta, isolation notes, and a promotion recommendation.
claims.verbatim: `cautilus evaluate skill-experiment` emits `cautilus.skill_clone_experiment_report.v1` with `variant_ran`, baseline-versus-variant delta, rubric match, source coverage delta, isolation notes, and a promotion recommendation.

claims.summary: The skill-experiment command does not install, mutate, or execute skills; host-owned runners provide the preserved baseline and variant outputs.
claims.verbatim: The command does not install, mutate, or execute skills; host-owned runners provide the preserved baseline and variant outputs.

claims.summary: Use --runtime fixture when the goal is cheap command-routing proof over fixture-backed runner results.
claims.verbatim: Use `--runtime fixture` when the goal is cheap command-routing proof over fixture-backed runner results.

claims.summary: --runtime fixture is not evidence that a model-backed skill execution is good; it is evidence that the Cautilus command surface, adapter, fixture translation, and packet evaluation path still line up.
claims.verbatim: It is not evidence that a model-backed skill execution is good; it is evidence that the Cautilus command surface, adapter, fixture translation, and packet evaluation path still line up.

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate report build builds a machine-readable evaluation report packet from explicit mode runs.
claims.verbatim: # build a machine-readable evaluation report packet from explicit mode runs

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate review prepare-input assembles a durable review packet around a report.
claims.verbatim: # assemble a durable review packet around a report

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate review build-prompt-input builds the product-owned meta-prompt packet.
claims.verbatim: # build the product-owned meta-prompt packet

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate review render-prompt renders the review prompt.
claims.verbatim: # render the review prompt

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate review variants runs every executor variant defined by an adapter.
claims.verbatim: # run every executor variant defined by an adapter

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate review variants can run an ad-hoc bounded review without first building a review packet.
claims.verbatim: # run an ad-hoc bounded review without first building a review packet

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate review feedback build normalizes a source-bound review outcome into review-learning evidence.
claims.verbatim: # normalize a source-bound review outcome into review-learning evidence

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate review feedback summarize summarizes selected review-learning packets by method family and disposition.
claims.verbatim: # summarize selected review-learning packets by method family and disposition

claims.summary: If you need a minimal valid report packet before review, run cautilus evaluate report build --example-input.
claims.verbatim: If you need a minimal valid report packet before review, run `cautilus evaluate report build --example-input`.

claims.summary: The report build example includes the minimum humanReviewFindings shape: { severity: concern, message: ..., path: optional }.
claims.verbatim: That example includes the minimum `humanReviewFindings` shape:

claims.summary: evaluate review variants writes a product-owned review-summary.json (cautilus.review_summary.v1) plus one per-variant cautilus.review_variant_result.v1 file.
claims.verbatim: `evaluate review variants` writes a product-owned `review-summary.json` (`cautilus.review_summary.v1`) plus one per-variant `cautilus.review_variant_result.v1` file.

claims.summary: A review variant can finish as passed, blocked, or failed; blocked runs carry machine-readable reason codes instead of prose-only abort text.
claims.verbatim: A variant can finish as `passed`, `blocked`, or `failed`; blocked runs carry machine-readable reason codes instead of prose-only abort text.

claims.summary: Local executor readiness failures such as missing auth are classified as blocked unavailable_executor results rather than negative review verdicts.
claims.verbatim: Local executor readiness failures such as missing auth are classified as blocked `unavailable_executor` results rather than negative review verdicts.

claims.summary: When at least one variant passes and another does not, the summary sets partialSuccess: true and lifts the passing outputs into successfulVariantOutputs.
claims.verbatim: When at least one variant passes and another does not, the summary sets `partialSuccess: true` and lifts the passing outputs into `successfulVariantOutputs`.

claims.summary: When --output-under-test is present, the generated review prompt switches into output_under_test mode and treats the referenced artifact as primary evidence.
claims.verbatim: When `--output-under-test` is present, the generated review prompt switches into `output_under_test` mode and treats the referenced artifact as primary evidence.

claims.summary: When --output-text-key is present, Cautilus also extracts that JSON narrative span into the rendered prompt so the judge can read the realized output directly.
claims.verbatim: When `--output-text-key` is present, Cautilus also extracts that JSON narrative span into the rendered prompt so the judge can read the realized output directly.

claims.summary: review feedback build writes cautilus.review_feedback.v1.
claims.verbatim: `review feedback build` writes `cautilus.review_feedback.v1`.

claims.summary: review feedback build preserves a source review reference plus normalized method, proposal, disposition, and note fields, while recording that normalization is based on the source review.
claims.verbatim: It preserves a source review reference plus normalized method, proposal, disposition, and note fields, while recording that normalization is based on the source review.

claims.summary: For accepted, narrowed, reframed, or rejected dispositions, include --proposal-id or --proposal-source-ref so the packet names the proposal that was reviewed.
claims.verbatim: For `accepted`, `narrowed`, `reframed`, or `rejected`, include `--proposal-id` or `--proposal-source-ref` so the packet names the proposal that was reviewed.

claims.summary: missing_critical may omit proposal evidence because it records that the method missed a proposal reviewers needed.
claims.verbatim: `missing_critical` may omit proposal evidence because it records that the method missed a proposal reviewers needed.

claims.summary: The review feedback disposition vocabulary is about review usefulness rather than pass/fail status.
claims.verbatim: Its disposition vocabulary is about review usefulness rather than pass/fail status.

claims.summary: review feedback summarize writes cautilus.review_feedback_summary.v1.
claims.verbatim: `review feedback summarize` writes `cautilus.review_feedback_summary.v1`.

claims.summary: review feedback summarize reads explicitly selected cautilus.review_feedback.v1 packets, counts review-useful dispositions overall and by method family, and keeps source-review refs in per-input records.
claims.verbatim: It reads explicitly selected `cautilus.review_feedback.v1` packets, counts review-useful dispositions overall and by method family, and keeps source-review refs in per-input records.

claims.summary: review feedback summarize does not discover active-run defaults or treat dispositions as evaluator pass/fail status.
claims.verbatim: It does not discover active-run defaults or treat dispositions as evaluator pass/fail status.

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate evidence prepare-input prepares normalized evidence input.
claims.verbatim: # prepare normalized evidence input

claims.summary: cautilus evaluate evidence bundle builds the evidence bundle.
claims.verbatim: # build the evidence bundle

claims.summary: cautilus improve is the improvement front door.
claims.verbatim: `cautilus improve` is the improvement front door.

claims.summary: Use cautilus improve only after the claim and eval proof surface are explicit enough that an improvement loop has something honest to improve against.
claims.verbatim: Use it only after the claim and eval proof surface are explicit enough that an improvement loop has something honest to improve against.

claims.summary: For app behavior claims, reports whose proofSummary.productRunnerProofReadiness is blocked cannot become improve inputs until runner-backed product proof is ready.
claims.verbatim: For app behavior claims, reports whose `proofSummary.productRunnerProofReadiness` is blocked cannot become improve inputs until runner-backed product proof is ready.
claims.edgeRationale: Improve-input gating depends on runner-backed product proof readiness from the evaluate surface (E1) before bounded improvement (I1).

claims.summary: cautilus improve prepare-input plus cautilus improve propose form the bounded one-shot improver.
claims.verbatim: The bounded one-shot improver:

claims.summary: cautilus doctor artifacts render-self-dogfood-html refreshes the static HTML view of the current checked-in self-dogfood bundle.
claims.verbatim: # refresh the static HTML view of the current checked-in self-dogfood bundle

claims.summary: cautilus doctor artifacts render-self-dogfood-experiments-html refreshes the static HTML comparison view of the current latest experiments bundle.
claims.verbatim: # refresh the static HTML comparison view of the current latest experiments bundle

claims.summary: The rendered HTML is read-only over summary.json, report.json, and review-summary.json; those JSON files remain the source of truth.
claims.verbatim: The rendered HTML is read-only over `summary.json`, `report.json`, and `review-summary.json`; those JSON files remain the source of truth.

claims.summary: claude plugins validate validates the checked-in Claude marketplace and plugin manifest.
claims.verbatim: # validate the checked-in Claude marketplace and plugin manifest

claims.summary: node ./scripts/release/check-codex-marketplace.mjs verifies Codex can discover the repo-local marketplace entry.
claims.verbatim: # verify Codex can discover the repo-local marketplace entry

claims.summary: Most underlying Node scripts are still runnable directly when a wrapper tool needs them without the cautilus binary.
claims.verbatim: Most underlying Node scripts are still runnable directly when a wrapper tool needs them without the `cautilus` binary.

claims.summary: Shipped runtime entrypoints such as cautilus discover live-targets and cautilus evaluate live are Go-owned now and should be called through the CLI instead of direct node scripts/agent-runtime/... paths.
claims.verbatim: Shipped runtime entrypoints such as `cautilus discover live-targets` and `cautilus evaluate live` are Go-owned now and should be called through the CLI instead of direct `node scripts/agent-runtime/...` paths.
claims.edgeRationale: The Go-owned runtime entrypoint boundary governs how the binary command surface (A1) exposes the live evaluate runtime (E1).

claims.summary: Only the remaining wrapper and packet-builder utilities listed should be invoked directly.
claims.verbatim: Only the remaining wrapper and packet-builder utilities below should be invoked directly.
